prompt:
You are Agent 2 of 4 working on the TaskFlow Pro project at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-6uerOt.

A previous developer implemented email notifications using an EventBus pattern in src/services/notification.service.ts.

Your task: Extend the notification system with SMS and webhook channels.

1. Review the existing notification service and EventBus implementation before writing any code.
2. Add an SMS channel handler that subscribes to the EventBus.
3. Add a webhook channel handler that subscribes to the EventBus.
4. Both new handlers must follow the same EventBus subscription pattern already in place.
5. Add tests for the new channel handlers.

Do NOT redesign the EventBus or the notification service — extend, do not replace.

parameters.task: Extend notification system with SMS and webhook channel handlers using existing EventBus pattern

parameters.summary: Add SMS and webhook channel handlers following the existing mixed EventBus + CallbackRegistry pattern
parameters.context: The existing NotificationService uses EventBus for order:created and CallbackRegistry for order:status-changed. New channel handlers need to subscribe to the same events.
parameters.rationale: Follow the existing pattern exactly to maintain consistency. Both new handlers subscribe to EventBus for order:created and CallbackRegistry for order:status-changed, mirroring the NotificationService. Each handler is a separate class in its own file, keeping concerns separated.

alternatives.option: Only use EventBus subscriptions, skip CallbackRegistry
alternatives.reason_rejected: Task says extend not replace; must follow existing patterns
Separate files follow single-responsibility and are easier to maintain

parameters.summary: Agent 2: Added SMS and webhook channel handlers with full test coverage (16 new tests, all passing)
parameters.detail:
Created two new notification channel services following the existing EventBus + CallbackRegistry dual pattern:

1. SmsChannelService (src/services/sms-channel.service.ts) - subscribes to order:created via EventBus and order:status-changed via CallbackRegistry, sends SMS messages (in-memory)
2. WebhookChannelService (src/services/webhook-channel.service.ts) - same subscription pattern, delivers structured payloads to registered webhook URLs

Tests: 7 SMS tests + 9 webhook tests, all passing. TypeScript compiles clean. No changes to existing code.
